1.1: The elder to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth—and not I only, but also all who have known the truth—
1.5: And now I ask you, dear lady—not as though writing to you a new commandment, but one we have had from the beginning—that we love one another.
1.3: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.
1.6: And this is love: that we walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, just as you have heard from the beginning, so that you should walk in it.
1.13: The children of your elect sister greet you.
1.2: because of the truth that remains in us, and will be with us forever:
1.4: I rejoiced greatly because I have found some of your children walking in the truth, just as we received commandment from the Father.
1.12: Though I have much to write to you, I do not wish to do so with paper and ink; but I hope to come to you and speak face to face, so that our joy may be made full.
1.8: Watch yourselves, so that you do not lose what we have worked for, but may receive a full reward.
1.7: For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not confess that Jesus Christ is coming in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the antichrist.
1.10: If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into your house and do not give him a greeting,
1.11: for the one who greets him shares in his evil works.
1.9: Everyone who goes on ahead and does not remain in the teaching of Christ does not have God; the one who remains in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.
